questions.prompt: Complete: "While my sister ___, I washed the dishes."
questions.explanation: "While" + past continuous gives the background action. "I washed" (past simple) is the completed action during that time: while she was sleeping.

questions.prompt: Complete: "I ___ never ___ sushi before. I tried it last night."
questions.explanation: "never" with present perfect describes a life experience up to now: I have never eaten sushi. The sentence then adds a specific past event (last night → past simple), showing the contrast.

options.text: She visited Paris two years ago.
She has visited Paris two years ago.
She have visited Paris two years ago.
She was visited Paris two years ago.

questions.explanation: "two years ago" is a specific past time marker. Use past simple, not present perfect: she visited Paris two years ago.

questions.prompt: Complete: "___ the film started when you arrived?"
questions.explanation: This asks about a completed event at a specific past moment. Past simple question: Did + subject + base verb. "Did the film start...?"
"already" between have/has and the past participle signals the present perfect: has already written. The result is visible now (Look!).

questions.prompt: Choose the correct pair: "They ___ TV when the lights ___ out."
questions.explanation: Past continuous (were watching) = background in progress; past simple (went) = the sudden interrupting event. This is the classic when-pattern.
